name: social-auth
summary: Native Apple + Google sign-in that goes THROUGH the backend seam: obtain the native credential, hand the idToken to backend.auth (Supabase signInWithIdToken OR your custom API's /auth/oauth).

env.name: EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_WEB_CLIENT_ID
env.note: Google OAuth *Web* client id (NOT the iOS/Android client id). Used as GoogleSignin webClientId so the returned idToken's audience matches what the backend verifies. Public by design — it identifies, it does not authorize.

iosNotes: Apple: enable the 'Sign In with Apple' capability/entitlement on the iOS target (CNG: add via app.json `ios.usesAppleSignIn: true` or the expo-apple-authentication config plugin — never edit ios/ by hand).
Google: add the iOS OAuth client's reversed-client-id URL scheme; the google-signin config plugin handles the native wiring. Apple sign-in is iOS-only — the button guards on Platform.OS.
postInstall: Set EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_WEB_CLIENT_ID (Google Cloud → Credentials → Web client id) AND add it to the EnvSchema in src/shared/lib/env.ts (process.env is read only there).

Configure the provider on your CHOSEN backbone: Supabase → enable Apple & Google in Auth → Providers (see server/supabase/README.md, no SQL); custom API → implement POST /auth/oauth that verifies the idToken server-side and issues your tokens (see server/api/routes.md).
iOS: enable Sign In with Apple entitlement (ios.usesAppleSignUp / config plugin); a dev build (not Expo Go) is required for both native modules.
Replace the placeholder buttons (src/ui/social-buttons.tsx) after the Claude Design pass.
